terms.definition: The degree to which a system maintains internally consistent order, resisting entropy
The thermodynamic tendency of an isolated system to move toward maximal disorder
A rapid non-linear change of state in a system once a control parameter crosses a critical threshold
An imposed boundary condition that restricts system degrees of freedom
The measurable variable representing constraint intensity within a domain
The parameter value at which a qualitative change (phase transition) occurs
Dimensionless constant describing how sharply an observable changes near the critical threshold
Applicable across qualitatively different subsystems without parameter retuning
Mathematical description of how connectivity emerges or collapses in networks as link density changes
A grouping of systems that share identical critical exponents near phase transitions
Mean-field approximation describing free-energy near criticality
The maximal rate at which information can be transmitted over a noisy channel with arbitrarily low error
Principle stating that entropy of an isolated system never decreases over time
A statistical method for testing whether one time series can predict another
The Ten Commandments as covenantal boundary conditions in Judeo-Christian theology
Resampling with replacement to estimate the sampling distribution of a statistic
